success_surface: Complex multi-step conditional logic spanning two sheets with validation-driven execution
effective_behaviors: Pre-execution exploration to validate assumed column positions against actual data layout
Step-by-step verification of conditional branch points before committing final writes
Reverse iteration when searching for values above a target position (closest-match semantics)
Explicit matching validation across sheets to prevent silent cross-referencing failures
Workbook state management including setting active sheet per instruction requirements
evidence: Agent ran exploratory code listing sheet names, max rows/columns, and header values before solution generation
Agent queried Column P values row-by-row to find first 'Yes'/'NA' entry before executing the main logic
Agent searched backwards from found row (range(first_yes_na_row - 1, 1, -1)) to find closest '100' above
Agent validated Cell F1 of Sheet A against all entries in Sheet B Column C before paste operation
Agent set wb.active = ws_a at end to ensure Sheet A becomes active upon completion

memory_items.title: Validate Column References Before Execution
memory_items.lesson: Do not assume column letter-to-index mappings are accurate without programmatic confirmation of actual cell values.
memory_items.generalization: Always query and log cell contents at presumed reference positions during an initial exploration phase. Confirm column letters match expected data types and headers before proceeding with read/write operations.

memory_items.title: Trace Conditional Branches Explicitly
memory_items.lesson: Complex multi-path logic requires verifying each condition independently to ensure the correct branch executes.
memory_items.generalization: Break down nested conditions into discrete validation steps. Log decision points (e.g., found/not-found, matched/not-matched) before committing results to enable error diagnosis and correct fallback behavior.

memory_items.title: Use Reverse Iteration for Upward Searches
memory_items.lesson: When searching for the nearest value above a target position, iterate backwards from the target toward the start.
memory_items.generalization: For 'closest match above/below' patterns, use range(start - 1, stop, -1) to find the most recent occurrence rather than searching forward from the beginning of the dataset.

memory_items.title: Verify Cross-Sheet Matches Before Referencing
memory_items.lesson: Cross-sheet references that depend on text matching require explicit validation to prevent silent failures.
memory_items.generalization: Before using values from one worksheet to determine actions in another, programmatically confirm the lookup key exists in both sheets. Handle mismatches gracefully with default behavior paths.

memory_items.title: Manage Workbook State Explicitly
memory_items.lesson: Some instructions require specific sheets to be active when the process completes.
memory_items.generalization: Set wb.active explicitly to the designated target sheet at the end of execution to preserve required workbook state for downstream users or tools.

edits.content:
## 6. Advanced Validation Patterns
- **Column Position Verification**: Before relying on assumed column indices, explore actual cell values at those positions to confirm alignment with expected headers/types.
- **Branch Path Logging**: For complex conditional workflows, log each decision point (found/not-found, matched/failed) to ensure correct branch execution and enable debugging.
- **Reverse Search Semantics**: When locating the nearest value above/below a target position, iterate backward from the target (start - 1 down to stop) to capture the closest occurrence.
- **Cross-Sheet Key Validation**: Validate lookup keys exist in both source and target sheets before performing cross-reference operations; implement graceful fallback paths for mismatches.
- **Workbook State Preservation**: Explicitly set `wb.active` to the instructed sheet at completion to maintain required open-worksheet state for downstream consumers.
